entries.text: Delete the deprecated clickup-sprint-planner plugin: remove its source tree (~900 LOC near-fork of shield/adapters/clickup, byte-identical clickup_client.py, zero tests, not in CI) and drop it from marketplace.json. Owner intends to do this later.

Enforce repo policies in CI: (a) a PR touching a plugin asset must bump the plugin version in marketplace.json; (b) pyproject version must agree with marketplace for python-server plugins; (c) changed plugin assets must ship an eval. Currently policy-by-review only (review finding #7).

Extract a shared forward_design_refs driver into shield/adapters/_common (skip-no-anchor / idempotent-skip / error+log loop is copy-pasted across jira/confluence/notion/clickup with drifted logging). Also consider unifying the 3 HTTP stacks (httpx/requests/urllib). Correctness fragility already fixed; this is the dedup half (review #11).

Reconcile skill-invocation namespace: commands reference skills as shield:domain:skill (path style) but registered skill names are flat (e.g. terraform-plan-analyzer), so the path-style strings are not actually invokable. Pick one convention repo-wide (review M2).

Notion adapter: document/justify last-write-wins on the single Design Links URL property (a second ref clobbers the first; only the key list accumulates), and bring test coverage to parity with clickup (currently contract+idempotency only). Adapter is v0.1.0 scaffold maturity (review M4).

Make writing-style globally available outside Shield. Portable today (single SKILL.md, no plugin-root/script deps). Recommended: generalized personal copy at ~/.claude/skills/writing-style/SKILL.md — keep the 4 principles + revision pass verbatim, generalize the scope guard to 'do not rewrite code blocks / generated/marker-wrapped regions / structured data', drop Shield-specific jargon (TRD §10, render_trd_section.py, sidecars, See-Also). Coexists with shield:writing-style (keeps Shield scope + CLAUDE.md contract). Trade-off: personal copy will not auto-update with the plugin (principles are stable). Alternatives: verbatim copy (carries jargon); symlink to plugin path (fragile, versioned path moves on update).
